channel.title: Free on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Free on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] C Double Free or Corruption — Freeing Already-Freed Memory Fix
item.description:
[Solution] C Double Free or Corruption — Freeing Already-Freed Memory Fix

A “double free or corruption” error occurs when your program attempts to free() a pointer that has already been freed, or when it frees a pointer that was never allocated on the heap. This corrupts the heap metadata used by the memory allocator, which can cause crashes, data corruption, or exploitable security vulnerabilities. The error message typically reads double free or corruption (fasttop) or double free or corruption (!prev).

item.title: [Solution] C Memory Leak — Valgrind Lost Bytes / Still Reachable Fix
item.description:
[Solution] C Memory Leak — Valgrind Lost Bytes / Still Reachable Fix

A memory leak occurs when your program allocates heap memory (via malloc, calloc, or realloc) but fails to free() it before the pointer is lost. Valgrind reports this as "definitely lost: N bytes in M blocks" or "still reachable: N bytes". While small leaks may be harmless in short-lived programs, large or repeated leaks exhaust memory and cause performance degradation or crashes.
